product_name: Pip and Posy: The New Friend
product_description_under_30_words: At the beach, Posy wakes to find Pip playing with new friend Zac — jealousy, then a small kind act reunites them.
ember_verdict: Not another feelings chart — this is the moment your child watches their best friend laugh with someone else. Posy fixes it herself, which gives you language for real playgroup wobbles without you stepping in as referee.
rank_rationale: #1 over #2: third-friend jealousy is the sharpest 34–36m social wobble and Posy’s self-repair beats Little Whale’s sharing lesson on specificity, even though both clear the friendship brief.
why_it_fits: Shows jealousy, inclusion and repair in peer play without adult rescue — the core 34–36m shift beyond emotion labelling.
caveats: Paperback is the value pick; board book ISBN differs. Pip & Posy titles are widely owned — borrow first.
gift_note: Warm story gift after a messy playdate — check they do not already own Pip & Posy titles.
ownership_note: Borrow or bring back out if nursery already has the Pip & Posy set.
review_quality_note: Consistent 4.8–4.9 across UK retailers for this Nosy Crow paperback.
preloved_signal_note: Picture books hold up well second-hand — check for scribbles on well-loved copies.
evidence_notes: Amazon UK primary smoke- and availability-checked buyable 2026-07-22. Nosy Crow age band 2–5 captured from publisher series page.

why_it_fits: Sharing and attention jealousy in peer contexts — matches turn-taking cluster without repeating 28–30m emotion-label books.
caveats: Newer title — fewer reviews than classics; strong editorial comparables on the listing.
gift_note: Good gift if you want something they have not already seen in a nursery book box.

product_description_under_30_words: Two furry friends battle over one chair, shouting 'It's mine!' until they choose to play together instead.
ember_verdict: The 'that's MINE' phase meets friend play. Short, shouty text mirrors real arguments, then pivots to shared play — handy after playdates where nobody wanted to swap.
rank_rationale: #3 over #4: the mine-shouting chair fight gives parents a same-day script after toy grabs, edging ahead of Sour Grape’s forgiveness arc which lands better once the row has cooled.
why_it_fits: Turn-taking and territory fights between peers — practical social rules, not naming happy/sad.
caveats: Hachette page lists ebook ISBN; paperback ISBN 9781444918335 confirmed via alternate.
gift_note: Small, funny gift — but check the shelf; this one circulates widely.

product_description_under_30_words: A grape who stockpiles grudges learns forgiveness after realising a birthday mix-up — and how sour holding on feels.
ember_verdict: When friend play goes wrong, nearly-threes can stay cross long after the toy is back. This is funny enough for repeat reads and gives you a light way into sorry and moving on.
rank_rationale: #4 over #5: forgiveness after a friend fallout is a harder nearly-three skill than competitive sharing, so Sour Grape ranks above Squirrels even though both model repair.
why_it_fits: Forgiveness and fairness in friendships — social repair, not basic emotion naming.
caveats: Some wordplay lands above toddler level; the visual story carries it. Food Group titles are nursery staples — borrow if you already own Bad Seed or Good Egg.
gift_note: Funny gift for a child who knows the Food Group characters.
ownership_note: High duplication risk if nursery already has Food Group books.
